title: GLP-1 Medications: Everything You Need to Know in 2026
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to GLP-1 receptor agonists for weight loss, including how they work, who qualifies, costs, and what to expect from treatment in 2026.
body:
GLP-1 receptor agonists have become the most talked-about weight loss medications in recent years. Originally developed for type 2 diabetes, drugs like semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy) and tirzepatide (Mounjaro, Zepbound) have shown remarkable results for weight management, with clinical trials demonstrating 15-20% body weight reduction over 68 weeks.

So how do these medications actually work? GLP-1 agonists mimic a natural hormone called glucagon-like peptide-1, which your body produces after eating. They slow gastric emptying, reduce appetite, and improve insulin sensitivity. The result is that patients feel fuller faster, experience fewer cravings, and naturally eat less without the constant willpower battle that derails most diets.

The big question for most people is cost. Brand-name GLP-1 medications like Wegovy can run $1,300+ per month without insurance. However, the compounded medication market has changed the game entirely. Telehealth providers like CoreAge Rx, Hims, and Ro now offer compounded semaglutide starting at $129-199/month, making these medications accessible to a much wider population. If you're considering GLP-1 therapy, we recommend comparing providers on our comparison tool to find the best price and fit for your needs.
author: Weight Loss Rankings Team

title: Hims vs Ro: Which GLP-1 Provider is Better?
excerpt: We compare Hims and Ro head-to-head on pricing, medications offered, state availability, user experience, and overall value for GLP-1 weight loss treatment.
body:
Hims and Ro are two of the biggest names in telehealth weight loss, and both offer compounded GLP-1 medications. But which one is actually better? We put them head-to-head across every dimension that matters to help you decide.

On pricing, Hims starts at $199/month for compounded semaglutide (with frequent promo codes bringing the first month down to $149), while Ro starts at $199/month as well. The key difference is that Ro also offers brand-name GLP-1 medications at $1,350/month for patients who prefer FDA-approved formulations. In terms of state availability, Hims covers all 50 states while Ro is limited to 20 states — a significant advantage for Hims if you live outside Ro's coverage area.

Where Ro pulls ahead is in the quality of care. Ro pairs each patient with a dedicated care team that includes nutrition coaching and ongoing physician oversight, earning it higher trust and effectiveness scores in our evaluation. Hims, by contrast, uses a text-only async consultation model that's fast and convenient but less personal. Our verdict: choose Hims if you want the lowest price and widest availability, choose Ro if you value comprehensive medical care and don't mind the geographic limitations. For an even better value, consider CoreAge Rx, which scored higher than both in our overall rankings.
author: Weight Loss Rankings Team

title: CoreAge Rx Review: Our Top-Rated Provider
excerpt: Our in-depth review of CoreAge Rx, the top-ranked GLP-1 provider on Weight Loss Rankings, covering pricing, medications, shipping, and overall experience.
body:
CoreAge Rx earned the top spot in our GLP-1 provider rankings with an overall score of 9.0/10, and after thoroughly evaluating their service, we can see why. They offer both compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide — one of the few providers to offer both — with pricing starting at $149/month and scaling to $349/month for the highest dose.

What sets CoreAge Rx apart is the combination of competitive pricing, broad availability, and verified quality. They're LegitScript certified, which means their pharmacy operations meet rigorous standards for safety and legitimacy. They ship to all 50 states with free 2-day delivery, and their 24/7 support team is genuinely responsive — not just a chatbot with limited hours.

The main downside is that CoreAge Rx only offers compounded medications, not brand-name Ozempic or Wegovy. For most patients, this is a non-issue since the active ingredient is identical, but if your insurance covers brand-name GLP-1s, you might save more going through a provider like PlushCare. For the vast majority of out-of-pocket buyers, though, CoreAge Rx delivers the best overall value in the market. Visit our full review page for the complete score breakdown and pricing details.
author: Weight Loss Rankings Team

title: How to Save Money on Semaglutide
excerpt: Practical strategies to reduce the cost of semaglutide treatment, from compounded alternatives to promo codes, savings programs, and insurance tips.
body:
Semaglutide is incredibly effective for weight loss, but the cost can be a barrier. Brand-name Wegovy lists at over $1,300/month, and even with manufacturer coupons, out-of-pocket costs remain steep for most people. The good news is that there are several legitimate ways to dramatically reduce what you pay.

The single biggest savings opportunity is switching from brand-name to compounded semaglutide. Compounded versions use the same active ingredient but are produced by 503B-registered pharmacies at a fraction of the cost. Providers like CoreAge Rx start at $149/month, Found at $129/month, and Hims at $199/month (often with $50-off promo codes for new patients). That's 85-90% less than brand-name pricing. Make sure any compounded provider you choose works with a LegitScript-verified or 503B-registered pharmacy.

Beyond compounding, check whether your insurance covers GLP-1 medications — coverage has expanded significantly in 2025-2026. PlushCare specializes in helping patients navigate insurance prior authorizations. You can also look into manufacturer savings programs (Novo Nordisk offers one for Wegovy), FSA/HSA accounts (Hims and several other providers accept these), and multi-month subscription discounts. Use our savings calculator to see exactly how much you could save based on your current medication and dose.
author: Weight Loss Rankings Team

title: FDA Updates on Compounded GLP-1 Medications
excerpt: The latest FDA guidance on compounded GLP-1 medications, what it means for patients and providers, and how to ensure you're getting safe, legitimate treatment.
body:
The FDA has been closely monitoring the compounded GLP-1 medication market as demand has exploded. In early 2026, the agency issued updated guidance clarifying the rules for 503A and 503B compounding pharmacies producing semaglutide and tirzepatide. Here's what patients need to know.

The key takeaway is that compounded GLP-1 medications remain legal and available when produced by properly licensed pharmacies during a drug shortage. The FDA maintains a shortage list, and as long as semaglutide or tirzepatide appear on it, compounding pharmacies can produce these medications. However, the FDA has cracked down on unlicensed operators and those making false claims about their products. Patients should verify that their provider works with a 503B-registered outsourcing facility or a state-licensed 503A pharmacy.

What does this mean for you? If you're currently using a compounded GLP-1 from a reputable provider, your treatment should continue uninterrupted. Look for providers that are transparent about their pharmacy partners and hold certifications like LegitScript verification. On our rankings, we flag providers that meet these safety standards. If you have concerns about your current provider's legitimacy, check the FDA's database of registered outsourcing facilities or consult our provider comparison tool to find a verified alternative.
author: Weight Loss Rankings Team
